headlines.headline: Japan Fires Missiles During Military Drills, Drawing China Rebuke
headlines.summary: Japan fired surface-to-ship missiles and sank an old warship in waters between the Philippines and Taiwan during major military exercises with US forces, prompting sharp criticism from Beijing.

headlines.headline: EU Suspends Methane Rules as Energy Crunch Intensifies
headlines.summary: The European Union is delaying enforcement of methane reporting penalties as it prioritizes energy security during a worsening supply crunch caused by the Iran war blockade.

headlines.headline: Turkish Defense Industry Showcases New Systems at SAHA 2026
headlines.summary: Turkish defense manufacturers presented new-generation systems at SAHA 2026 in Istanbul, highlighting Türkiye indigenous military production capabilities.

headlines.headline: Kazakh President Calls for Military Reform on Defender Day
headlines.summary: President Tokayev congratulated citizens on Defender of the Fatherland Day, reaffirming commitment to strengthen national defense amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Russian Military Logistics Hub Near Moscow Hit in 347-Drone Attack
headlines.summary: Ukraine launched a massive drone blitz targeting a military logistics hub near Moscow, striking as Russia tightens security ahead of Victory Day celebrations.

headlines.headline: BAE Systems confident on growth as Iran war drives defense orders
headlines.summary: Britain's largest defense contractor says it's on track for 9-11% earnings growth this year as the Iran conflict keeps orders flowing, highlighting how geopolitical tensions are boosting Western defense industries.

headlines.headline: Trump sees swift end to Iran war as Tehran reviews US peace proposal
headlines.summary: Tehran is considering a U.S. peace proposal that would formally end the conflict while leaving unresolved key demands that Iran suspend its nuclear program and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump expressed optimism about a quick resolution.

headlines.headline: Paraguay president visits Taiwan as China ramps up diplomatic pressure
headlines.summary: President Santiago Peña arrived in Taiwan leading a business delegation, reaffirming Paraguay's diplomatic ties as one of only 12 countries worldwide that still recognize Taiwan. China has been intensifying efforts to isolate the island diplomatically.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence Links Trump Dinner Shooting Suspect to Iran War Motive
headlines.summary: A DHS preliminary assessment found the suspect had multiple grievances, with the Iran conflict potentially a motivating factor. The report dated April 27 was leaked as the war continues to create domestic security concerns.

perspectives.angle: US Intelligence: Iran Conflict Motivated Trump Assassination Attempt
perspectives.summary: DHS identified the US-Israel war with Iran as potential motive for the man accused of attempting to assassinate President Trump at a White House reporters gala last month.

headlines.headline: Two Russian Drones Crash in NATO Member Latvia, Damaging Oil Facility
headlines.summary: Two drones entered Latvia from Russian territory and crashed, with one damaging an oil storage facility. The incident raises NATO concerns about spillover from the Ukraine war as Baltic states remain on high alert.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Accuses Russia of Violating Kyiv-Proposed Ceasefire
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy said Russia conducted dozens of assaults, airstrikes and drone attacks, calling it Moscows obvious spurning of peace. The failed ceasefire comes ahead of Russias May 9 Victory Day celebrations.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine accuses Russia of violating ceasefire proposed by Kyiv
perspectives.summary: President Zelenskiy says Russia carried out dozens of assaults, air strikes and drone attacks, demonstrating Moscow's "obvious spurning" of peace efforts.

headlines.headline: Russia to Buy Foreign Currency as Iran War Oil Windfall Boosts Revenues
headlines.summary: Moscow will purchase forex for the first time since the Ukraine war began, as Middle East conflict-driven oil prices have boosted Russian crude export revenues. The move signals economic resilience despite sanctions.

perspectives.angle: Russia resumes forex purchases as Iran war oil windfall boosts revenues
perspectives.summary: Russia will buy foreign currency for the first time since the Ukraine war began, as surging oil prices from the Middle East conflict have increased Moscow's crude export revenues.

headlines.headline: Russia Warns Foreign Diplomats to Evacuate Kyiv Ahead of Victory Day
headlines.summary: Moscow warned diplomatic missions to evacuate staff from Kyiv in case of retaliatory strikes if Ukraine disrupts May 9 Victory Day commemorations. The threat escalates tensions ahead of the symbolic Russian holiday.

perspectives.angle: Russia Warns Diplomats to Evacuate Kyiv Before May 9
perspectives.summary: Russia warned foreign diplomatic missions to evacuate staff from Kyiv if Moscow launches mass strikes in response to Ukrainian attempts to disrupt Victory Day commemorations.

headlines.headline: Australia forces gas giants to reserve 20% for domestic market
headlines.summary: Australia will require LNG exporters to reserve 20% of their gas for the domestic east coast market to avert supply shortfalls and lower energy bills amid global energy disruptions from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China condemns Eswatini for being "kept and fed" by Taiwan
headlines.summary: Beijing used unusually strong language against Taiwan's remaining African ally, saying Eswatini's leaders are being "kept and fed" by Taipei as diplomatic tensions rise.

headlines.headline: Zelensky announces legislation to legalize private military companies in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Ukraine is preparing laws to establish private military companies, creating postwar opportunities for veterans and expanding Kyiv's role in the global security sector.

headlines.headline: ASEAN leaders gather in Philippines amid Middle East energy crisis
headlines.summary: ASEAN summit in Cebu focuses on energy security and Middle East tensions, with fuel import-dependent Southeast Asian economies facing significant challenges from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China Expands Influence Across Asia as Iran War Creates Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: The Iran war has left China's neighbors appealing for energy help, handing Beijing the regional sway it has long sought. Countries across Asia are turning to China as fuel shortages bite.

headlines.headline: US Massing Troops Near Taiwan to Deter Chinese Aggression
headlines.summary: America is building up military forces near Taiwan as a deterrent against potential Chinese adventurism during the Iran crisis. The deployment signals US commitment to the Indo-Pacific despite Middle East demands.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Attacks Spill Into NATO Territory in Romania
headlines.summary: Four years of war in Ukraine is taking a toll on neighboring Romania as Russian drones repeatedly cross into NATO territory. Residents live with the constant threat of weapons of war entering their airspace.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine war spills into NATO territory as drones hit Romania
perspectives.summary: Four years of battle in Ukraine is taking a toll on neighboring Romanians, with drones from the conflict crossing into NATO territory even as the war itself has not crossed the border.

headlines.headline: US Private Payrolls Post Largest Gain in 15 Months Despite Middle East Uncertainty
headlines.summary: US private payrolls rose significantly in April, pointing to continued labor market stability even as the Iran conflict clouds the economic outlook. The data suggests resilience in the American economy.

headlines.headline: US fires on Iranian oil tanker amid war pressure
headlines.summary: US military forces fired on an Iranian oil tanker as the Trump administration continues to pressure Tehran for a deal to end the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Navy Disables Iranian Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
perspectives.summary: US military disabled an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday for allegedly violating the blockade of Iranian ports, marking continued tensions even as diplomatic talks progress.

headlines.headline: Japan's Trump Supporters Lose Faith Amid Iran War Chaos
headlines.summary: Longtime Japanese supporters of Trump are questioning their allegiance as Iran war disruptions and troop withdrawal plans raise concerns about US-Japan security ties.

headlines.headline: India Sidelined by Iran War as Regional Diplomacy Unravels
headlines.summary: The Iran-Israel conflict has undermined New Delhi's delicate balancing act in the Middle East, leaving India without leverage as major powers reshape regional dynamics.

headlines.headline: Taiwan's Silicon Shield Exposed by Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: The Strait of Hormuz disruption reveals vulnerabilities in Taiwan's economic power, as its technology sector depends on stable global supply chains now under threat.

headlines.headline: Russian Commander Claims Western Funding for Mali Militants
headlines.summary: A Russian Africa Corps helicopter commander claims illegal armed groups attempting a coup in Mali receive Western funding, amid ongoing instability in the Sahel.

headlines.headline: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel struck Beirut for the first time since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah, targeting a commander of the militant group's elite Radwan force in the city's southern suburbs.

perspectives.angle: Israel strikes Beirut targeting Hezbollah commander
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces conducted the first strike on Beirut's southern suburbs since the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took effect on April 17, targeting a senior Hezbollah figure.

headlines.headline: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
headlines.summary: North Korea stated it is not bound by any treaty on non-proliferation of nuclear weapons, as state media KCNA reported Pyongyang continues to reject international pressure and sanctions to dismantle its nuclear program.

headlines.headline: North Korea revises constitution to drop reunification references
headlines.summary: North Korea has revised its constitution to define its territory as bordering South Korea and remove references to reunification, codifying leader Kim Jong Un's push to treat the two Koreas as separate states.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Drops Reunification Goal in Constitutional Shift
perspectives.summary: Pyongyang has removed references to "national reunification" and redefined inter-Korean borders in its revised constitution, signaling a potential move toward peaceful co-existence with South Korea.

headlines.headline: Rubio to meet Pope Leo as Trump continues attacks on pontiff
headlines.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican, in a potentially fraught encounter as President Donald Trump has continued disparaging attacks on the Catholic leader over the Iran war.

perspectives.angle: Top Vatican cardinal calls Trump attacks on Pope Leo strange
perspectives.summary: A senior Vatican Cardinal called US President Donald Trump's disparaging attacks on Pope Leo over the Iran war "strange," a day before Secretary of State Marco Rubio is due to hold talks with the pope.

perspectives.angle: Vatican cardinal calls Trump attacks on Pope Leo strange before Rubio visit
perspectives.summary: A senior Vatican cardinal criticized President Trumps public attacks on Pope Leo over the Iran war as strange, one day before US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to meet with the pontiff. Tensions between Washington and the Vatican have escalated over differing approaches to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies at 87
headlines.summary: The billionaire media mogul who revolutionized cable news with the launch of CNN in 1980 has died. Turner also owned the Atlanta Braves and was a champion yachtsman, leaving an indelible mark on both media and sports.

perspectives.angle: CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
perspectives.summary: Media mogul Ted Turner, who revolutionized TV news by launching CNN in 1980, has died at age 87. He pioneered the modern 24-hour news culture.

headlines.headline: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier Toward Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: France moved its Charles de Gaulle carrier strike group to the Red Sea in preparation for a potential mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes as Washington and Tehran negotiate a possible peace deal.

headlines.headline: Japan Nikkei Hits Record High Above 62,000 on Middle East Peace Hopes
headlines.summary: Japans benchmark Nikkei 225 surged past 62,000 for the first time ever, driven by strong tech earnings and growing optimism about a potential US-Iran peace deal. Asian stocks broadly rallied on the news.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Rejects ICJ Jurisdiction in Guyana Oil Territory Dispute
headlines.summary: Venezuela told the UN International Court of Justice it has no jurisdiction over its century-old dispute with Guyana concerning the oil-rich Essequibo region. Venezuela claims the territory was fraudulently taken in the colonial era.

headlines.headline: ECB Rate Hike Chances Rise Amid Inflation Pressures
headlines.summary: European Central Bank board member Piero Cipollone said the probability of an ECB rate hike has increased as inflation pressures remain elevated. The Iran war has complicated the global easing cycle.

headlines.headline: US Counterterrorism Strategy Adds Offensive Cyber Operations
headlines.summary: The Trump administration unveiled a new counterterrorism strategy that includes offensive cyber operations against groups deemed threats to US interests, marking a significant expansion of American counterterrorism tools.

headlines.headline: Pentagon AI Adoption Gives Cyber Criminals Nation-State Powers
headlines.summary: Pentagon leaders praise agentic AI tools that compress weeks of work into hours, but security experts warn the same tools are giving cyber criminals capabilities previously reserved for nation-states.

headlines.headline: Trump Counterterrorism Strategy Brands Europe Terrorism Incubator
headlines.summary: The new US counterterrorism strategy accuses Europe of fostering terrorism through mass migration while expanding domestic focus beyond Islamic extremism to include other threats.

headlines.headline: Japan Nikkei Surges Past 61,000 on Iran Deal Optimism
headlines.summary: Japanese stocks hit a record high as markets reopened after holidays, with optimism growing around a potential US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Japan fires missile in joint drill with US allies in Philippines near South China Sea
headlines.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired an anti-ship missile during exercises with US, Australian and Philippine forces, demonstrating growing military cooperation in waters facing the contested South China Sea.

perspectives.angle: Japan fires anti-ship missiles in joint drill with US and allies near South China Sea
perspectives.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired a Type 88 anti-ship missile during a joint exercise with US, Australian, and Philippine forces, sinking a decommissioned ship in waters between the Philippines and Taiwan. China condemned the drills.

headlines.headline: Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump signals war may be ending
headlines.summary: Iran is reviewing a US peace proposal that would formally end the war, though key American demands on Iran's nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz remain unaddressed.

perspectives.angle: Iran Reviewing US Peace Proposal as Both Sides Edge Toward Deal
perspectives.summary: Iran is reviewing a one-page US proposal that could end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, requiring Tehran to suspend nuclear enrichment while Washington releases billions in frozen assets. The White House awaits Tehran's response as diplomatic momentum builds.

headlines.headline: G7 trade ministers discuss critical minerals as US-EU tariff tensions strain unity
headlines.summary: Trade ministers from the Group of Seven sought common ground on securing critical mineral supplies dominated by China, but fresh US tariff threats against European cars risked straining alliance unity.

perspectives.angle: G7 Trade Ministers Seek Unity on Critical Minerals Supply
perspectives.summary: G7 trade ministers meeting in Paris sought common ground on securing critical mineral supplies dominated by China, as fresh US tariff threats against EU cars risked straining unity.

headlines.headline: Gulf Economies Face Long-Term Hit from Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: The ongoing Iran-US-Israel war is dealing a sustained blow to Gulf economies, with Iranian strikes on gas fields and shipping disruptions threatening the region economic transformation plans.

perspectives.angle: Gulf states face uncertainty as Iran war reshapes regional alliances
perspectives.summary: Middle East expert warns that Gulf monarchies will have mixed feelings about the US after the Iran war, as the conflict exposes growing rift between UAE and Saudi Arabia over energy quotas and regional security.

headlines.headline: Fed Officials Warn Supply Chain Risks Fuel Inflation Concerns
headlines.summary: Federal Reserve officials said the US-backed war with Iran is raising the risk of sustained inflation shock, with high oil prices and growing concerns about global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: Pope to Visit Canary Islands, Key Migrant Entry Point
headlines.summary: Pope Leo will visit the Canary Islands, major entry points for migrants braving dangerous Atlantic waters, as part of a tour of Spain next month, the Vatican announced.

headlines.headline: A Year Into Papacy, Pope Leo Finds His Voice on World Stage
headlines.summary: Pope Leo marks his first year leading the Catholic Church with a higher public profile, having grown more outspoken on global affairs and drawn the ire of US President Donald Trump.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo Marks First Year with Growing Global Profile
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo marks his first year leading the Catholic Church with higher public profile and outspoken stance on world affairs, drawing criticism from Trump over nuclear weapons position.

headlines.headline: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
headlines.summary: Pyongyang says the US and other countries are "tarnishing the atmosphere" at the UN NPT review conference by raising concerns about its nuclear weapons program.

headlines.headline: Russia cancels Victory Day celebrations in Crimea over Ukraine drone threat
headlines.summary: Russian occupation authorities in Crimea cancelled May 9 Victory Day events citing security concerns amid Ukrainian drone strikes targeting the peninsula.

headlines.headline: Israeli forces launch new incursion into Syrias Daraa province
headlines.summary: Israeli forces carried out an incursion in the Yarmouk Basin region in western Daraa countryside late Wednesday, marking continued Israeli operations in southern Syria.

headlines.headline: Russias YJ-20 hypersonic missile threatens US carrier dominance
headlines.summary: SCMP analyzes Chinas YJ-20 hypersonic sea-based missile, a highly maneuverable weapon that could sink aircraft carriers with a single hit and is nearly impossible to intercept.

headlines.headline: UAE Rejects Iran Criticism of US Defense Partnerships
headlines.summary: The UAE stated its international defense partnerships are a sovereign matter, dismissing Iranian claims that Abu Dhabi cooperation with the US threatens Iranian security.

headlines.headline: China's Wang Yi Meets Iranian Foreign Minister Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi met Iran's Abbas Araghchi in Beijing, calling for efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The meeting comes a week before Trump's scheduled visit to meet Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Iran foreign minister meets Chinese counterpart days before Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Iranian FM Abbas Araqchi held talks with China's Wang Yi in Beijing, underscoring close ties between the two countries just a week before US President Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Iran's Araghchi Meets China's Wang Yi in Beijing
perspectives.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held talks with Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Beijing, seeking China's continued support as Trump visits next week. Wang called for reopening the Strait of Hormuz "as soon as possible" while Araghchi stressed Iran wants a "fair and comprehensive agreement" with the US.

perspectives.angle: China Hosts Iranian Foreign Minister as Beijing Seeks Role in Hormuz Negotiations
perspectives.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi met with his Chinese counterpart in Beijing as China positions itself as a key mediator in the US-Iran conflict, with Trump pausing the Hormuz escort operation pending a possible peace deal.

headlines.headline: UK Advises Hantavirus Cruise Ship Returnees to Self-Isolate
headlines.summary: British health authorities advised two people who returned from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius to self-isolate. The ship departed for Spain's Canary Islands after three evacuations.

headlines.headline: Australia Commits $7 Billion to Boost Fuel Stockpiles Amid Supply Concerns
headlines.summary: Australia will expand onshore fuel reserves to at least 50 days of supply, creating a government reserve of 1 billion liters to shield against future supply shocks following Middle East disruptions.

headlines.headline: China Enables Iran's Shahed Drone Supply Lines, Nikkei Analysis Finds
headlines.summary: A Nikkei Asia analysis examines how China facilitates Iran's Shahed drone production, which has been used extensively by Russia in Ukraine and by Iranian-backed groups across the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus cruise ship heads to Canary Islands amid health emergency
headlines.summary: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak departed for Spains Canary Islands after 3 passengers were evacuated. Four people have died from the outbreak, with at least 8 confirmed infections aboard the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.

perspectives.angle: Deadly Hantavirus Outbreak Spreads on Antarctic Cruise Ship
perspectives.summary: Three passengers have died and one remains in intensive care from a hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship sailing from Argentina to Antarctica. WHO is investigating the source as the ship awaits evacuation near Cape Verde.

headlines.headline: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier to Red Sea for Potential Hormuz Mission
headlines.summary: France moved its carrier strike group to the Red Sea as part of planning for a possible mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz, urging Washington and Tehran to consider a diplomatic proposal given the global economic impact of their competing blockades.

headlines.headline: Euro Zone Services Activity Contracts for First Time in Nearly a Year
headlines.summary: Euro zone services activity slumped in April, contracting for the first time in almost a year as weakening demand and the Middle East war weighed on consumer-facing sectors. The deterioration adds to concerns about Europe economic resilience.

headlines.headline: Germany Weighs Bigger Stake to Block Italian Takeover of Commerzbank
headlines.summary: German government members are examining a drastic step to take a bigger stake in Commerzbank to fend off UniCredit unwanted bid, viewing the lender as strategic. The move reflects growing European banking tensions.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Deal Framework Emerging After Trump Pauses Hormuz Operation
headlines.summary: The US and Iran are moving toward a negotiation framework after Trump halted a maritime escort mission in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is reviewing a US proposal while key sticking points over uranium enrichment remain unresolved.

perspectives.angle: Macron doubts Trump Hormuz operation, says France will not participate
perspectives.summary: French president criticizes unilateral US move to reopen Strait of Hormuz, stressing Europe will only support a coordinated Iran-US agreement. The France-US rift continues to widen.

headlines.headline: Modi BJP Seizes Historic Control of West Bengal, Extending Grip to 70% of Indian States
headlines.summary: Narendra Modi ruling coalition now controls roughly 70% of India state legislatures after BJP stunning conquest of West Bengal, ending three consecutive terms of Mamata Banerjee Trinamool Congress. The victory represents the conquest of the party final frontier.

headlines.headline: Central Banks Pause Rate Cuts as Iran War Roils Markets
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has forced major central banks to hold interest rates steady in April, disrupting global monetary easing efforts amid inflation pressures and market volatility.

perspectives.angle: Iran war pauses global central bank easing push
perspectives.summary: Major central banks have put interest rate cuts on hold as the Iran war creates inflation pressures and market volatility. The conflict has disrupted global energy markets and complicated monetary policy decisions worldwide.

perspectives.angle: South African Markets Rally on Iran War De-escalation Hopes
perspectives.summary: South African stocks, bonds, and rand strengthened after Trump signaled possible peace deal with Iran, boosting risk appetite for emerging market assets.

perspectives.angle: Barclays Bets No Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 as Iran War Fuels Inflation
perspectives.summary: Barclays joined a growing list of brokerages predicting no Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, citing prolonged high energy prices from the Iran war that are likely to keep inflation elevated.

headlines.headline: Pussy Riot Protesters Swarm Russia's Venice Biennale Pavilion
headlines.summary: Activists from Pussy Riot and FEMEN staged a protest against Russia's presence at the Venice Biennale, releasing colored smoke and swarming the pavilion in pink balaclavas.

headlines.headline: FBI Searches Virginia Senate Leader's Office in Corruption Probe
headlines.summary: The FBI executed a search warrant at Virginia Senate Leader Louise Lucas's office as part of an ongoing corruption investigation.

headlines.headline: U.S. NATO exit would force Europe into fiscal creativity and faster defense spending
headlines.summary: A Reuters commentary examines how a potential U.S. withdrawal from NATO would require Europe to spend more and faster than the $350 billion already pledged by 2035, suggesting low-cost tech adoption and new mutual financial schemes.

perspectives.angle: US NATO exit would force Europe into major defense spending surge
perspectives.summary: A potential US withdrawal from NATO would require European nations to spend more and faster than the $350 billion already pledged by 2035, according to a Reuters Breakingviews analysis.

headlines.headline: Poland warns Russia shifting from low-cost recruits to professional sabotage cells
headlines.summary: Poland raised alarms about Russia transitioning from using low-cost recruits to deploying professional sabotage cells, following what the prime minister called an unprecedented act of sabotage with explosions in November 2025.

headlines.headline: China Advances Submarine Detection Tech with Quantum SQUID Sensors
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists report progress on gravity-detecting SQUID sensors capable of identifying US nuclear submarines by their mass signature, potentially threatening US undersea supremacy.

headlines.headline: Eurovision Crisis Deepens Over Israel-Gaza Controversy
headlines.summary: Multiple countries are boycotting or threatening to withdraw from Eurovision 2026 over Israel participation, with artists including Massive Attack and Kneecap calling for fans to boycott the contest.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Near Deal on Uranium Transfer
headlines.summary: Reports indicate Washington and Tehran are closing in on an agreement that would see Iran give away its enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader peace framework.

headlines.headline: Gulf's Trade Lifeline Hangs on Two UAE Ports Amid Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: The UAE ports of Fujairah and Khor Fakkan have become critical chokepoints for Gulf trade as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. Grain offloading and crude loading continue at Fujairah while regional supply chains reroute through the eastern UAE facilities.

perspectives.angle: Oman reports two injured as residential building in Bukha targeted amid Hormuz tensions
perspectives.summary: A residential building in Oman's Bukha region along the Strait of Hormuz coastline was targeted, injuring two people, as regional tensions escalate following US-Iran clashes in the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: America massing troops near Taiwan to deter Chinese aggression
headlines.summary: The US is significantly increasing its military presence near Taiwan amid heightened tensions with Beijing. The buildup comes as China continues provocative actions in the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: Putin is losing his grip on Russia
headlines.summary: An analysis suggests Vladimir Putin has led Russia into a strategic dead-end, with growing internal pressures and military setbacks in Ukraine undermining his authority. The war has exposed fundamental weaknesses in Russias military and economic model.

headlines.headline: Russia losing influence in Africa as setbacks mount in Mali
headlines.summary: Russian forces are facing significant setbacks in Mali, highlighting Moscows declining influence across Africa as local partners reassess their relationships.

headlines.headline: EU backs new compensation mechanism for victims of Russian war
headlines.summary: EU ambassadors approved a new compensation mechanism for victims of Russias war, though no country besides Ukraine has ratified the International Claims Commission.

perspectives.angle: EU Formally Backs Nuremberg-Style Tribunal to Prosecute Russian Leaders for War of Aggression
perspectives.summary: EU ministers voted to join a Council of Europe court that will prosecute Russian leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine, the same charge used against Nazi leaders at Nuremberg. The tribunal aims to hold Putin and other top officials accountable.

headlines.headline: Hungary returns seized Ukrainian bank assets in diplomatic breakthrough
headlines.summary: Hungary returned seized Ukrainian bank assets in what Zelensky called a civilized step, marking an improvement in bilateral relations.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Fall, Markets Rise on Reports of US-Iran Peace Deal Progress
headlines.summary: Brent crude dropped to $97 a barrel while European stock markets rallied after reports that the US and Iran are close to a memorandum of understanding to end the war. The deal would declare an end to hostilities and start negotiations on reopening the strait.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia and UAE Drift Toward Open Rivalry Over Oil Policy and Regional Influence
headlines.summary: Competition between the Gulf powers has turned into tension over energy quotas, regional conflicts and differing visions for the Middle East, as the UAE leaves OPEC and Saudi Arabia asserts dominance.

perspectives.angle: Saudi-UAE Rift Deepens Over Energy Policy and Regional Conflicts
perspectives.summary: Tensions between Gulf allies mount over OPEC energy quotas and diverging approaches to regional conflicts, raising questions about Gulf unity.

perspectives.angle: Iran Demands Explanations From Saudi Arabia and UAE After Chinese-Made Drone Shot Down
perspectives.summary: Tehran seeks answers after a Chinese-manufactured drone was intercepted, raising questions about regional drone transfers and potential Gulf state involvement.

headlines.headline: Washington Post: Iran War Deepens US Political Divide Over Israel Alliance
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has fractured the traditional bipartisan consensus on US-Israel ties, with both Democrats and Republicans now fiercely debating the alliance on campaign trails and in Congress.

headlines.headline: Bloomberg: UK Faces Disastrous Breakup as Four Nations Drift to Separatist Rule
headlines.summary: Polls suggest three of the four UK nations may soon be ruled by separatist parties dedicated to ending the union, a historic shift that could unravel centuries of political unity.

headlines.headline: Vietnam and India Seek Closer Ties to Hedge Against Superpower Dependence
headlines.summary: Both nations pursue deeper strategic partnership as they look to diversify away from reliance on major powers amid growing US-China rivalry in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: US dollar stuck in range as markets watch Strait of Hormuz developments
headlines.summary: FX strategists in a Reuters poll say developments in the US-Iran war will steer the dollar in the near term, with the currency expected to stay range-bound before weakening later this year.

headlines.headline: Syria Dismantles Hezbollah-Linked Assassination Cell
headlines.summary: Syrian authorities arrested a Hezbollah-linked cell accused of plotting to assassinate government officials. The operation signals Syria's continued efforts to assert control and address Iranian proxy operations on its soil.

headlines.headline: Türkiye-Kenya Sign Defense Cooperation Agreement at Istanbul Expo
headlines.summary: Türkiye and Kenya signed a defense cooperation agreement during the SAHA 2026 International Defense and Aerospace Exhibition in Istanbul, expanding Ankara's defense partnerships in Africa.

headlines.headline: Freedom Flotilla Vessels Continue Gaza Mission Through Greek Waters
headlines.summary: Four vessels from the Freedom Flotilla Coalition are sailing through Greek territorial waters en route to Gaza, defying the blockade amid the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Local Elections Could End Starmer's Troubled Premiership
headlines.summary: British voters are casting ballots in local elections that could hasten the end of Prime Minister Keir Starmer's troubled term, as the hard-right Reform UK party eyes significant gains.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship to Sail to Spain; Rare Andes Strain Confirmed
headlines.summary: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak with close to 150 people on board was due to head to Spain, while South Africa confirmed it identified the Andes strain of the virus that can spread among humans. Three passengers have died.

perspectives.angle: Second hantavirus case confirmed on cruise ship after three deaths
perspectives.summary: The WHO confirmed a second hantavirus case on the MV Hondius cruise ship anchored off Cape Verde. Three passengers have died and WHO warns human-to-human transmission may be occurring among close contacts.

headlines.headline: Ukrainians Trapped in Frontline City of Oleshky
headlines.summary: Around 2,000 civilians remain trapped in the Russian-occupied city of Oleshky, cut off from food and medicine supplies for months. Residents face a "Road of Death" if they attempt to flee, with heavy mining and attacks making evacuation routes extremely dangerous.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Civilians Trapped in Frontline City as Humanitarian Crisis Deepens
perspectives.summary: Residents in Oleshky, a Russian-occupied city on the east bank of the Dnipro river, report being cut off from food and medicine for months. Civilians face danger from mined roads dubbed the "Road of Death" while trying to evacuate the besieged city.

headlines.headline: Kim Ju Ae's Fashion Signals North Korean Succession
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un's daughter is being styled as his successor through sophisticated Western-style clothing and formal outfits, analysts say. Her evolving fashion, including luxury items like a ,900 Dior jacket, contrasts sharply with tightening controls on ordinary citizens' clothing.

perspectives.angle: Kim Jong Un Daughter Fashion Signals Succession Preparation
perspectives.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un's daughter Kim Ju Ae is being styled in increasingly sophisticated outfits mirroring her mother and featuring luxury Western brands, signaling she may be groomed as the country's next leader amid tightening controls on ordinary citizens.

headlines.headline: China Calls for Comprehensive Ceasefire in Iran War
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi urged a complete ceasefire in the US-Iran conflict during talks with Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi in Beijing. The meeting comes one week before Trump's scheduled visit to China for a summit with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: Anadolu Agency: Beijing Extends Support to Iran Amid US Conflict Ahead of Trump Visit
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi emphasized international concern over restoring normal passage through the Strait of Hormuz during talks with Iranian counterpart Araghchi.

headlines.headline: Japan Pivots Indo-Pacific Strategy Toward Southeast Asia
headlines.summary: PM Takaichi used a visit to Vietnam to update Japan’s Indo-Pacific strategy, putting energy security, critical minerals and ASEAN resilience at its center.

headlines.headline: South Korea Proposes Integrated Military Academy for Cross-Branch Training
headlines.summary: President Lee Jae Myung aims to boost integrated operational capability by having future military officers develop cross-branch expertise from the start.

headlines.headline: Italy and Turkey Deepen Defense Ties Through TB3 Drone Integration
headlines.summary: Italy and Türkiye are strengthening NATO’s southeastern flank through defense cooperation, including TB3 drone integration and air defense system discussions.

perspectives.angle: UAE Buys 10 Brazilian Military Aircraft as BRICS Defense Ties Deepen
perspectives.summary: The UAE has purchased 10 C-390 Millennium transport aircraft from Brazil, strengthening military cooperation within the BRICS bloc. The deal signals Abu Dhabis push to diversify defense partnerships beyond Western suppliers.

headlines.headline: Russian Strikes Kill Over 20 Ahead of Rival Ceasefire Declarations
headlines.summary: Russian attacks killed more than 20 people across Ukraine, including 12 in Zaporizhzhia, just before both Moscow and Kyiv announced separate unilateral ceasefires. Ukraine declared an open-ended truce from May 6, while Russia announced a ceasefire for May 8-9 for Victory Day celebrations.

headlines.headline: Çin Dışişleri Bakanı İranlı Mevkidaşına Kapsamlı Ateşkes Çağrısı Yaptı
headlines.summary: Çin Dışişleri Bakanı Wang Yi, İranlı mevkidaşı Abbas Araghçi ile görüşerek iki aydan uzun süren savaştan derin endişe duyduğunu ve kapsamlı bir ateşkes gerektiğini belirtti. Görüşme, Trump'ın Pekin ziyaretinden hemen önce gerçekleşiyor.

headlines.headline: İran ABD ile Kapsamlı Anlaşma İstediğini Açıkladı
headlines.summary: İran Dışişleri Bakanı Abbas Araççi, ABD ile savaşı sona erdirecek müzakerelerde "adil ve kapsamlı bir anlaşımı" kabul edeceğini belirtirken, Trump anlaşma yolunda "büyük ilerleme" olduğunu söyledi. Trump, Hürmüz Boğazı'ndaki eskort operasyonunu da durdurdu.

headlines.headline: Rusya Ukrayna'nın Tek Taraflı Ateşkesini İhmal Edip İHA'larla Saldırdı
headlines.summary: Rusya, Kyiv'in ilan ettiği tek taraflı ateşkesi görmezden gelerek düzineyle insansız hava aracı gönderdi. Ukrayna yetkilileri, saldırıların Karadeniz kıyısındaki liman kentleri hedef aldığını ve en az 22 kişinin öldüğünü bildirdi.

headlines.headline: ABD'li Rubio Vatikan'da Papa Leo ile Dini Özgürlük Görüşecek
headlines.summary: ABD Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio, Papa Leo ile Thursday günü görüşeceğinden ve dini özgürlük konusunu ele alacağından bahsetti. Görüşme, Trump'ın Papa'yı İran-İsrail savaşı eleştirileri nedeniyle hedef almasının ardından geliyor.

headlines.headline: Brezilya Lula Trump ile Örgütlü Suçla Mücadele Konuşacak
headlines.summary: Brezilya Devlet Başkanı Lula, bu haftaki Trump görüşmesinde örgütlü suçla mücadele anlaşmasını gündeme getirecek. Başkan Yardımcısı Alckmin, ülkelerin transnasyonel örgütlü suçla mücadelede önemli işbirliği yapabileceğini belirtti.

headlines.headline: UAE OPEC'ten Ayrıldı: Küresel Petrol Düzeni Yeniden Yazılıyor
headlines.summary: BAE'nin OPEC'ten ani ayrılışı, kartelin zayıflamasına ve küresel petrol fiyatları üzerinde daha fazla oynaklığa neden olabilir. ABD'ye daha yakınlaşan BAE, kendi üretim artışını serbest bırakırken OPEC'in fiyat belirleme gücünü azaltıyor.

headlines.headline: ABD Ordusu 'Hackathon' ile Silah Sistemlerini Entegre Ediyor
headlines.summary: ABD Ordusu, Lockheed Martin, Boeing, Palantir ve Anduril gibi savunma yüklenicilerini bir araya getirerek silah sistemlerinin birbiriyle konuşmasını sağlamayı amaçlıyor. Ukrayna'dan alınan dersler, hızlı entegrasyonun savaş alanında kritik olduğunu gösteriyor.

headlines.headline: Tayvan'ın Güney Çin Denizi Ziyareti: Çin'in 'Gri Bölge' Oyunlarından Biri Daha
headlines.summary: Tayvan Sahil Güvenlik Bakanı'nın Tartışmalı Spratly Adaları'ndaki Taiping (Itu Aba) ziyareti, Çin'in kendi kıyı güvenlik taktiklerini andırıyor. Tayvan, askeri olmayan birimlerle egemenlik iddiasını sürdürürken gerilimi artırmaktan kaçınıyor.

headlines.headline: İngiltere'den Güney Çin Denizi'nde Özgürlük Navigasyonu
headlines.summary: Kraliyet Donanması'nın HMS Spey gemisi, Çin'in 'on çizgi' iddiasına karşı Spratly Adaları çevresinde özgürlük navigasyonu operasyonu gerçekleştirdi. 2016'daki uluslararası tahkimi yok sayan Çin'in adalarda askeri varlığı sürüyor.

headlines.headline: Burkina Faso junta secretly detained investigative journalist, Reporters Without Borders says
headlines.summary: Reporters Without Borders reports that Burkina Faso's military junta secretly held journalist Atiana Serge Oulon and up to 40 others in a makeshift detention facility in Ouagadougou, subjecting them to abuse, since June 2024.

headlines.headline: US, allies propose separate e-commerce duty moratorium as WTO talks deadlocked
headlines.summary: The US and allies plan to proceed with their own moratorium on e-commerce duties if Brazil and Turkey continue blocking extension of the global deal at WTO negotiations. The 1998 moratorium banning digital tariffs faces expiry.

headlines.headline: Japonya İkinci Dünya Savaşı Sonrası İlk Kez Güney Çin Denizi'nde Savaş Rolü Üstleniyor
headlines.summary: Japonya, 1400 askerle Filipinler'deki Balikatan tatbikatına katılarak savaş rolü üstlenen ilk kez II. Dünya Savaşı sonrası Çin'in kapısında askeri güç projeksiyonu yapıyor. Japonya-Filipinler savunma işbirliği derinleşiyor.

headlines.headline: UAE Intercepts Iranian Missiles and Drones as Attacks Continue
headlines.summary: The UAE Disaster Management Authority confirmed its defense systems successfully intercepted missile and drone attacks from Iran, urging residents to remain in safe locations amid ongoing hostilities.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu joins world leaders condemning Iranian attacks on UAE
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu phoned UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed to condemn Iranian missile and drone attacks on the Emirates, highlighting regional solidarity amid ongoing Iran-US tensions.

perspectives.angle: UAE Air Defences Engage Missiles and Drones as Iran Denies Attacking
perspectives.summary: The UAE defence ministry said its air defences were dealing with missile and drone attacks, while Iran's joint military command denied carrying out any operations against the UAE. The incident comes amid heightened regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Russia Cuts Mobile Internet in Moscow and St. Petersburg Ahead of Victory Day Parade
headlines.summary: Russian authorities shut down mobile internet services in major cities days before the annual May 9 Victory Day celebrations, citing security concerns amid heightened fears of Ukrainian drone attacks on the scaled-back military parade.

headlines.headline: US Approves $373.6 Million JDAM Weapons Sale to Ukraine
headlines.summary: The US State Department approved a weapons sale including extended-range Joint Direct Attack Munition systems to Ukraine. The package includes over 1,500 JDAM tail kits to enhance Ukraine's precision strike capabilities.

headlines.headline: Palo Alto Networks Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Enterprise Firewalls
headlines.summary: A critical buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2026-0300) in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS has been exploited in targeted attacks against enterprise firewalls. The flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges. Patches expected May 13.

headlines.headline: Two Russian hypersonic missile scientists convicted of treason, sentenced to 12 years
headlines.summary: Two Russian physicists involved in hypersonic missile research were convicted of treason and sentenced to 12.5 years each in a closed-door trial. The cases are part of a broader crackdown on academics amid heightened wartime security.

perspectives.angle: Two Russian Hypersonic Scientists Sentenced to 12 Years for Treason
perspectives.summary: Two Russian physicists involved in hypersonic missile research were convicted of treason and sentenced to 12-and-a-half years in prison each. The convictions continue a pattern of treason cases against Russian scientists working on sensitive defense technologies.

headlines.headline: South Korea inflation hits near two-year high on Middle East conflict oil surge
headlines.summary: South Korea's consumer prices rose 2.6% in April, the fastest pace in nearly two years, driven by soaring oil prices from the Iran war. The data raises pressure on the central bank to consider rate hikes.

headlines.headline: Rubio calls UN Hormuz resolution a test of the body's utility
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged China and Russia not to veto a US-proposed UN resolution demanding Iran stop attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a test of whether the UN remains relevant.

headlines.headline: Rubio Claims US Achieved Iran Operation Objectives
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the US has achieved its objectives in the Iran campaign despite not securing Tehran's enriched uranium, adding that protecting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz is a separate defensive operation. He confirmed 10 civilian sailors have died in the strait during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Rubio: Israel-Lebanon Peace Deal Achievable Despite Hezbollah
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said peace between Israel and Lebanon was achievable but identified Hezbollah as a key obstacle. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions and separate negotiations involving Israel's northern border.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Aims to Raise $295M from Privatization This Year
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Julia Svyrydenko announced Ukraine plans to raise approximately 13 billion hryvnia ($295 million) from privatizing state assets this year, as Kyiv seeks to strengthen its economy amid ongoing war with Russia.

headlines.headline: CICA offers Asian alternative to NATO for Middle East security architecture
headlines.summary: The little-known security forum CICA says it provides architecture for de-escalation in the Middle East, allowing members to project soft power amid Iran war. China leads diplomatic efforts to reduce regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UK Sanctions Russia for Recruiting Migrants to Fight in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Britain imposed sanctions on 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine, as well as those producing drones for the war effort.

headlines.headline: Trump Broke OPEC, He May Regret It
headlines.summary: Military campaigns in Venezuela and Iran have weakened OPEC more than anyone expected, but the White House may ultimately face consequences for disrupting the cartel.

perspectives.angle: Trump Broke OPEC, but the Consequences May Prove Costly
perspectives.summary: US military actions in Venezuela and Iran have weakened OPEC beyond expectations, but the strategic gains may come at the price of reduced influence over global oil markets and heightened regional instability.

headlines.headline: Asia Stranded Seafarers Suffer as Iran War Drags On
headlines.summary: An unprecedented crisis is unfolding for 20,000 seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf as the Strait of Hormuz closure leaves crews trapped on ships with no clear way out.

headlines.headline: Indian Rupee Hits Record Low as US-Iran Tensions Spark Economic Fears
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee slid to a record low as the fragile truce in the Middle East came under strain after new US-Iran attacks over the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting economic spillover risks from the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Indian Rupee Hits Record Low as US-Iran Strains Mount
perspectives.summary: The Indian rupee slid to a record low as a fragile Middle East truce came under strain after the US and Iran launched new attacks to wrestle for control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global energy supplies.

headlines.headline: Pacific Island Nations Face Fuel Shortages as Iran War Disrupts Supply Chains
headlines.summary: The Asian Development Bank is coordinating regional support for Pacific nations struggling with soaring fuel costs, as the Iran conflict strains global energy distribution networks.

perspectives.angle: Iran war fuel shock undercuts value of Japan airline frequent flyer miles
perspectives.summary: JAL and ANA fuel surcharges are diminishing the appeal of reward miles as oil prices surge from Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Tokyo-London airfares have climbed 90% since conflict began.

headlines.headline: US Energy Exports Surge While Domestic Gas Prices Climb
headlines.summary: US exporters are filling global energy gaps caused by the Iran war, but rising domestic fuel costs are raising questions about prioritizing exports over domestic supply.

perspectives.angle: US Energy Exports Surge as Domestic Fuel Prices Draw Scrutiny
perspectives.summary: US exporters have helped plug key global energy shortages caused by the Iran war, but sharply rising domestic fuel costs are raising questions about the merits of sending so much overseas while pump prices climb at home.

headlines.headline: European Gas Prices Dip as US Moves to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Dutch gas prices edged lower as US efforts to weaken Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz provided hope for restored shipping lanes.

perspectives.angle: Dutch Gas Prices Edge Lower as US Moves to Open Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: European natural gas prices fell as US efforts to weaken Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz raised hopes for improved energy supply flows through the critical shipping lane.

headlines.headline: US Army fires Tomahawk from Typhon system for first time in Philippines drills
headlines.summary: The US Army successfully fired a Tomahawk missile from its Typhon Mid-Range Capability launcher during Balikatan exercises in the Philippines, marking a significant milestone for the new missile system deployed in the Indo-Pacific.

perspectives.angle: US fires first Tomahawk from Philippines during Balikatan exercises
perspectives.summary: The US Army conducted its first live-fire test of a Tomahawk missile from the Philippines using the Typhon mid-range missile system. The test comes amid heightened tensions with China over military exercises in the Pacific.

headlines.headline: ASEAN leaders under pressure to address economic fallout from Iran conflict
headlines.summary: The escalating impact of the Iran war on Southeast Asia is expected to dominate this week's ASEAN Summit in the Philippines, as regional leaders grapple with economic spillover effects.

headlines.headline: Iran-linked hackers use ChatGPT for up to 700,000 cyberattacks daily, UAE warns
headlines.summary: UAE officials said artificial intelligence is being used for reconnaissance, data collection, and identifying system vulnerabilities, marking a significant escalation in state-sponsored cyber warfare capabilities.

headlines.headline: 30 Democratic lawmakers ask Rubio to reveal details of Israel nuclear program
headlines.summary: A letter from 30 Democratic members of Congress requests transparency on Israel's nuclear capabilities, arguing the US cannot develop a coherent nonproliferation policy for the Middle East without this information.

headlines.headline: Two Killed in Leipzig After Car Drives Into Crowd in Eastern Germany
headlines.summary: A car drove into a crowd in Leipzig city center, killing two people and injuring at least 22 others. German authorities arrested a 33-year-old German citizen; prosecutors say there is no indication of a political or religious motive.

perspectives.angle: Two killed as car rams crowd in Leipzig, Germany
perspectives.summary: A car drove into a crowd in the eastern German city of Leipzig, killing two people and injuring others. The driver was arrested; authorities have not yet determined if it was an accident or an attack.

headlines.headline: UAE Reports Iranian Attacks for Second Day as Hormuz Tensions Flare
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates said it came under Iranian missile and drone attacks for a second consecutive day, while Iran denied responsibility. The escalation comes as the US and Iran remain locked in a standoff over the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: US Sinks Iranian Boats as Tehran Attacks UAE and Strait Shipping
perspectives.summary: Iran launched attacks on UAE and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz while the US destroyed Iranian boats attempting to interfere with "Project Freedom." The fragile ceasefire continues to unravel as both sides exchange fire.

headlines.headline: U.S. Gasoline Prices Top $4.50 Per Gallon Amid Iran War Disruptions
headlines.summary: The U.S. national average retail gasoline price surpassed $4.50 a gallon for the first time since July 2022, as the U.S.-Iran conflict continues to disrupt oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The spike comes ahead of the summer driving season.

perspectives.angle: US Restaurant Sales Drop as Iran War Pushes Gasoline Prices Higher
perspectives.summary: Major US restaurant chains including Wingstop and Domino's reported weaker-than-expected sales growth as soaring gasoline prices from the US-Iran conflict forced customers to cut back on discretionary spending.

headlines.headline: Russian Court Orders Billionaire to Hand Farming Giant Stake to State
headlines.summary: A Russian court ruled that Vadim Moshkovich, billionaire founder of agriculture firm Rusagro, must transfer a stake to the state. The ruling marks another high-profile asset seizure as the Kremlin tightens control over strategic industries.

perspectives.angle: Russian Court Seizes Billionaires Stake in Farming Giant for State
perspectives.summary: A Russian court ruled billionaire Vadim Moshkovich must transfer his stake in Rusagro to the state in another high-profile asset grab targeting oligarchs.

headlines.headline: China pushes 10,000-card computing clusters in AI infrastructure race
headlines.summary: Chinese cities and tech giants are racing to build massive AI computing clusters with up to 10,000 accelerator cards, betting that scale will drive faster model training and lower costs in the global AI competition.

headlines.headline: Former NATO commander warns US-Iran in game of chicken over Hormuz
headlines.summary: Retired General Wesley Clark says the US and Iran are locked in a dangerous game of chicken over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides testing the limits of a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Rubio declares War Powers Act unconstitutional
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio argues the War Powers Act is 100 percent unconstitutional, staking out an aggressive position on executive war powers amid ongoing Iran operations.

headlines.headline: Russian Second-Largest Oil Refinery Halts After Ukrainian Drone Strike
headlines.summary: Russia's Kirishi refinery, the country's second-largest, halted processing after Ukrainian drone attacks damaged three of its four crude distillation units. The strike represents a significant blow to Russia's refining capacity.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian FSB Facility in Occupied Crimea
perspectives.summary: Drones reportedly hit an FSB security service facility in occupied Crimea, marking a significant strike against Russian intelligence infrastructure on the annexed peninsula.

headlines.headline: Tigray Party Restores Pre-War Government in Threat to Ethiopia Peace Deal
headlines.summary: Tigray's main political party reasserted control over the northern Ethiopian region, violating a key provision of the deal that ended the civil war with the federal government. The move threatens the fragile peace agreement.

headlines.headline: Germany's Merz Sticks With Coalition Despite Policy Wrangling
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz stayed with his centre-left coalition partners despite weeks of wrangling over tax, welfare and health reforms that have raised questions about the future of his government.

headlines.headline: China Condemns Expanded US Sanctions on Cuba as Illegal
headlines.summary: China urged Washington to immediately end its embargo and sanctions on Cuba, calling the expanded measures "illegal" and saying they violated international relations norms.

headlines.headline: Romanian Government Collapses After No-Confidence Vote
headlines.summary: Lawmakers toppled Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan's pro-EU government in a no-confidence vote, putting at risk the country's sovereign debt ratings and access to EU funds. The collapse marks a significant political shift with potential implications for European unity.

perspectives.angle: Romanian Government Collapses in No-Confidence Vote
perspectives.summary: Romania's pro-EU coalition government fell after losing a no-confidence vote, with socialists and far-right parties joining forces to topple the prime minister, unleashing fresh political uncertainty in the EU member state.

headlines.headline: Boko Haram Attack Kills 23 Soldiers in Chad
headlines.summary: Islamist Boko Haram militants attacked a military base in Chad's Lake Chad region, killing at least 23 security personnel and injuring 26 others. The attack marks one of the deadliest assaults on Chadian forces in recent months.

perspectives.angle: Boko Haram kills 23 Chadian soldiers in Lake Chad attack
perspectives.summary: Boko Haram militants attacked a military base on Lake Chad, killing at least 23 troops and wounding dozens more. President Deby vowed to eradicate the group despite claiming it had no sanctuary in Chad last year.

headlines.headline: China Fireworks Factory Blast Kills 26
headlines.summary: An explosion at a fireworks factory in China's Hunan province killed at least 26 people and injured 61, flattening buildings. President Xi Jinping ordered a thorough investigation into the disaster at China's fireworks capital.

headlines.headline: Indian Chief Minister Refuses to Resign After BJP Victory
headlines.summary: Mamata Banerjee, swept from power by Modi's BJP in West Bengal state elections, said she had "not been defeated" and would not resign. The unprecedented political twist follows BJP's historic win in the key Indian state.

headlines.headline: Nine Workers Die in Colombia Coal Mine Explosion
headlines.summary: A coal mine explosion in Colombia's Cundinamarca province killed nine workers and injured six others, the national mining agency reported. The incident occurred weeks after risk control recommendations were issued for the site.

headlines.headline: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeachment heads to plenary vote amid coercion claims
headlines.summary: Duterte-Carpio's camp has accused administration allies of pressuring legislators to support impeachment or face consequences, as the case moves toward a full chamber vote.

headlines.headline: Russian and Qatari foreign ministers discuss Strait of Hormuz situation
headlines.summary: Lavrov and his Qatari counterpart discussed developments around the strategic waterway amid ongoing US-Iran tensions and maritime security concerns.

headlines.headline: API reports large crude oil draw of 8.1 million barrels
headlines.summary: The American Petroleum Institute estimated significant inventory declines, with crude down 8.1 million barrels, indicating tightening supply dynamics.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz rules out snap elections or minority government
headlines.summary: Friedrich Merz dismissed speculation about early elections, committing to stable governance as his new administration takes shape.

headlines.headline: Gas prices expected to cool drastically in five states
headlines.summary: After last week's spike, gas prices in five states are projected to decline significantly, offering relief to consumers amid regional volatility.

headlines.headline: US Industries Split Over Trump Tariff Probe on Factory Capacity
headlines.summary: Domestic US industries are urging higher tariffs to curb exports from China and other countries with excess factory capacity, while import-dependent and agriculture groups advocate a more cautious approach to new trade restrictions.

headlines.headline: UK Sanctions Network Exploiting Migrants and Supplying Russian Drone Industry
headlines.summary: Britain sanctioned 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine and produce drones for the conflict. The measures aim to disrupt Russia's drone production capabilities.

perspectives.angle: UK Sanctions Russian Recruitment of Migrants to Fight Against Ukraine
perspectives.summary: Britain unveiled sanctions against 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine and producing drones for the conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump and Xi to Discuss Taiwan in Upcoming Talks
headlines.summary: Taiwan is likely to be a topic of conversation between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in upcoming discussions, according to U.S. officials.

headlines.headline: Turkey Ramps Up Steel Dome Air Defense System Production
headlines.summary: Turkish defense firm Aselsan will ramp up delivery of parts for Turkey's integrated multi-layer Steel Dome air defense system in 2026, strengthening the country's indigenous air defense capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Turkey Accelerates Delivery of Steel Dome Air Defense System Components
perspectives.summary: Turkish defense firm Aselsan will ramp up deliveries of parts for Turkey's integrated multi-layered "Steel Dome" air defense system in 2026, strengthening national air defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: German FM Backs Israel Presence in South Lebanon
headlines.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said Israel has every right to be in south Lebanon, calling the ongoing operation necessary, but warned of the damages of war.

headlines.headline: NATO Needs Policies for Sharing AI-Generated Intel
headlines.summary: A NATO official said new policies are needed to share AI-generated intelligence across all 32 alliance members, as current arrangements rely on workarounds and exceptions.

headlines.headline: Alberta Separatists Submit Signatures for Independence Referendum
headlines.summary: Alberta separatists delivered nearly 302,000 signatures to force a provincial referendum on leaving Canada, exceeding the 178,000 threshold. Premier Danielle Smith says she will proceed if signatures are verified, though she personally opposes separation. Indigenous groups are challenging the move in court.

perspectives.angle: Alberta separatists submit 300,000 signatures for independence referendum
perspectives.summary: The Stay Free Alberta group delivered over 300,000 signatures to trigger a potential independence referendum in October. A legal challenge by First Nations groups argues the separation would violate their treaty rights, with a court decision expected later this month.

headlines.headline: Supreme Court Restores Telehealth Access to Abortion Pill Mifepristone
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court temporarily blocked a lower court ruling that had restricted mifepristone access, restoring telehealth and mail-order prescriptions for the abortion pill. The order remains in effect for a week while the court considers the case, which Louisiana brought challenging FDA safety rules.

headlines.headline: US-Bahrain Draft UN Resolution Seeks Sanctions Over Iran Strait of Hormuz Attacks
headlines.summary: The US and Bahrain are pushing a UN Security Council draft resolution that could authorize sanctions or force against Iran if it fails to halt attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: U.S. and Gulf Allies Propose UN Resolution Threatening Iran Over Strait Closure
perspectives.summary: The United States and Gulf allies have proposed a UN Security Council resolution threatening Iran with sanctions unless it halts attacks on ships, stops charging illegal tolls, and discloses all mine placements in the Strait of Hormuz. China and Russia previously vetoed a weaker resolution.

headlines.headline: Israel signals return to war in Gaza as ceasefire talks stall
headlines.summary: Israel has expanded military control over 60% of Gaza while hardening disarmament demands, with analysts warning the Mladenov peace proposal is deliberately geared toward rejection to justify renewed assault. Senior Israeli military officials say the best time to defeat Hamas is now.

headlines.headline: Thailand cancels 25-year oil pact with Cambodia after border clashes
headlines.summary: Thailand scrapped a 2001 agreement for joint offshore energy exploration with Cambodia, forcing Phnom Penh to pursue UN arbitration. The move follows armed conflict between the neighbors last year.

headlines.headline: Ukraine corruption probe shadows top US negotiator
headlines.summary: Ukraine National Security Council chief Rustem Umerov faces renewed scrutiny over leaked tapes linking him to a major corruption scandal, even as he leads delicate peace negotiations with Washington and Moscow.

headlines.headline: Brazil Embraer Eyes New Middle East Defense Deals After UAE Order
headlines.summary: Brazilian aerospace company Embraer expects new defense aircraft deals in the Middle East, including government-to-government procurements, following a landmark C-390 military plane order from the UAE.

perspectives.angle: UAE Orders Embraer Millennium Airlifter in Middle East Breakthrough
perspectives.summary: The UAE has ordered the Embraer KC/C-390 Millennium airlifter, marking the Brazilian aerospace company first major defense sale in the Middle East region.

headlines.headline: UAE Under Iranian Missile and Drone Attack as US Says Ceasefire Holds
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates reported Iranian missile and drone attacks even as Washington maintained a shaky US-Iran ceasefire was intact. US forces exchanged fire while attempting to keep the Strait of Hormuz open for shipping.

headlines.headline: US Trade Deficit Widens as AI Investment Boom Drives Imports Higher
headlines.summary: The US trade deficit widened in March as an artificial intelligence investment surge pulled in imports, offsetting increased exports including crude oil shipments amid the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Russian Strike Kills Five at Ukraine Gas Production Facilities
headlines.summary: A Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure killed five people overnight at Naftogaz gas production facilities. Zelenskiy denounced Moscow's ceasefire offer as cynical amid continuing strikes.

perspectives.angle: Russian Missile Attack Kills Five at Ukrainian Gas Production Facilities
perspectives.summary: A Russian overnight missile and drone attack on Ukrainian gas production facilities killed five people, including two rescue workers, as Kyiv and Moscow exchanged rival ceasefire proposals around Victory Day commemorations.

headlines.headline: Russian Pavilion Reopens at Venice Biennale Despite Controversy
headlines.summary: Russia returned to the Venice Biennale for the first time since its 2022 invasion of Ukraine, opening its pavilion amid bitter controversy and protests from Ukrainian officials and activists.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Chief: Iran Ceasefire Holds Despite Hormuz Clash
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth affirmed the ceasefire with Iran remains in effect, even as U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire near the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. is conducting temporary maritime operations to protect commercial vessels.

perspectives.angle: US Declares Offensive Phase of Iran War Complete as Ceasefire Holds
perspectives.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced that Operation Epic Fury, the US-Israeli offensive against Iran launched in February, is complete. The fragile ceasefire continues despite Iranian attacks on UAE and ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, where the US military is attempting to reopen commercial shipping lanes.

headlines.headline: South Korea rejects Trump call to join Hormuz mission
headlines.summary: Seoul says UN mandate and global coalition required before joining US-led operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian fire on ships.

perspectives.angle: South Korea rebuffs Trump's call to join Hormuz mission after ship fired on
perspectives.summary: South Korea indicated it requires a UN mandate and global coalition before joining Trump's "Project Freedom" to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, after Iran reportedly fired on a South Korean cargo vessel.

headlines.headline: Russian fiber-optic FPV drones reach Kramatorsk
headlines.summary: Advanced Russian drones with fiber-optic guidance have appeared in Kramatorsk, posing a new threat to one of Ukraine key strongholds in Donetsk Oblast.

headlines.headline: US Service Sector Cools Sharply as Energy Prices Bite
headlines.summary: U.S. services sector growth slowed for a second month in April, with new orders dropping the most in three years as energy prices from the Iran conflict pushed input costs to their highest since late 2022.

headlines.headline: India Infrastructure Giant L&T Reports Profit Drop as Middle East War Disrupts Projects
headlines.summary: Larsen & Toubro posted a 3.1% fall in quarterly profit as the Middle East conflict disrupted the infrastructure giants projects in the region, highlighting economic spillover from the Iran war.

perspectives.angle: IMF Chief Warns of Global Economic Damage if Middle East War Extends into 2027
perspectives.summary: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warned that inflation is already picking up and the global economy could face a much worse outcome if the Middle East war drags into 2027 with oil prices hitting around $125 per barrel.

headlines.headline: Pacific Nations Face Fuel Shortages as Iran War Strains Energy Markets
headlines.summary: Countries across Asia are uniting to support nations struggling with high costs due to soaring fuel and energy prices in the wake of the Iran war, with Pacific states particularly exposed to supply disruptions.

perspectives.angle: Guyana President: Energy Investment Insufficient as Iran War Strains Global Supply
perspectives.summary: President Irfaan Ali warned that the gap between energy supply and demand is widening as the Iran war enters its third month, driving up oil and gas prices amid supply shortages.

headlines.headline: US Warns of Visa Sanctions on China Over Repatriation Slowdown
headlines.summary: A senior Trump administration official warned that Washington was prepared to increase travel restrictions on China if Beijing does not speed up repatriation of Chinese nationals in the US illegally.

perspectives.angle: US prepared for visa sanctions on China over migrant repatriation slowdown
perspectives.summary: A senior Trump administration official warned that China is slowing efforts to repatriate Chinese nationals in the US illegally. Washington is prepared to increase travel restrictions if Beijing does not reverse course.

headlines.headline: Iraq offers deep discounts for tankers braving Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iraq has offered to cut crude oil prices by more than 25% for shipping firms willing to risk transit through the Strait of Hormuz, as hundreds of ships remain stranded off Dubai.

perspectives.angle: Iraq offers steep oil discounts for Hormuz transit
perspectives.summary: Iraq is offering significant price discounts to buyers willing to transit the Strait of Hormuz despite ongoing conflict, attempting to maintain exports through the contested waterway.

headlines.headline: Turkish company signs deal to export 100,000 kamikaze drones
headlines.summary: Turkish defense firm Pasifik Teknoloji announced a major agreement to sell tens of thousands of FPV kamikaze drones, highlighting Turkey's growing defense export industry.

headlines.headline: Trump Looks Forward to Xi Summit as US-China Tensions Mount
headlines.summary: President Trump said he looks forward to meeting China's Xi Jinping later this month, calling the summit "very important" amid tensions over trade, technology, and the Iran war that has disrupted energy flows.

perspectives.angle: China urges US to drop trade probe ahead of Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Beijing calls on Washington to abandon Section 301 investigations targeting 16 trading partners for structural excess capacity, as a key Trump-Xi summit approaches that could reshape bilateral relations.

headlines.headline: Trump's Germany Troop Cuts Show Limits of NATO Efforts to Keep US on Board
headlines.summary: European officials have been working to convince Trump to keep the US in NATO despite tensions over the Iran war, but his abrupt move to cut US forces in Germany signals such efforts have limits.

perspectives.angle: German foreign minister confident on NATO deterrence despite US troop cuts
perspectives.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul expressed confidence there would be no shortfall in NATO conventional deterrence capability in Europe after Washington announced troop drawdowns in Germany.

headlines.headline: Armenia Hosts Historic EU Summit as It Charts Course Away from Russia
headlines.summary: Armenia hosted its first EU summit in Yerevan, marking a deepening of ties with Europe. French President Macron highlighted the country's shift away from Russian influence amid tensions with Moscow.

headlines.headline: China Signals Readiness to Export J-35A Fighter Jet to Pakistan
headlines.summary: State media showed a J-35A model with AVIC logo and English lettering, suggesting China's first export of its new stealth fighter may go to Pakistan amid growing defense ties.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence: Iran No Closer to Nuclear Weapon Despite War
headlines.summary: US intelligence agencies estimate Tehran remains about a year away from being able to build a nuclear weapon, despite weeks of fighting, suggesting the conflict has not significantly degraded Iran's nuclear timeline.

headlines.headline: Finland Joins Ukraine-Led Corpus Defense Procurement Coalition
headlines.summary: Finland has joined the Ukraine-led Corpus defense procurement coalition, aiming to strengthen defense procurement coordination and supply chain resilience among partner nations.

headlines.headline: EU Hails Leap Forward in Ties With Russia's Ally Armenia
headlines.summary: The EU and Armenia laid the groundwork for closer ties at a summit in Yerevan, marking another step in the former Soviet republic's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

headlines.headline: US defense contractor Leidos raises 2026 forecast on strong military demand
headlines.summary: Defense contractor Leidos Holdings raised its profit and revenue forecast for 2026, citing strong demand for IT and military intelligence services amid ongoing global conflicts and the Iran-US war.

headlines.headline: Reuters wins two Pulitzer Prizes for Trump investigations and Meta exposé
headlines.summary: Reuters won Pulitzer Prizes for national reporting on Trump's campaign of political retribution and for beat reporting exposing Meta's harmful AI and advertising practices.

headlines.headline: Armenia Hosts Historic EU Summit as Yerevan Shifts Away from Russia
headlines.summary: Armenia hosts first-ever European Union summit in Yerevan, marking a deepening of ties that signals a strategic pivot away from Moscow.

perspectives.angle: Armenia hosts EU summit in pivot away from Russia
perspectives.summary: Armenia held its first-ever EU-Armenia summit in Yerevan, signaling a historic shift toward Europe as public sentiment turns against Russia. Moscow has responded with cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns ahead of June elections.

headlines.headline: Sudan Accuses Ethiopia of Drone Attacks, Recalls Ambassador
headlines.summary: Sudan military accuses Ethiopia and UAE of drone strikes on Khartoum, recalling its ambassador from Addis Ababa as regional tensions escalate.

headlines.headline: US Secret Service Shoots Gunman Near White House
headlines.summary: Secret Service returned fire at an armed individual near the White House, wounding him and briefly triggering a lockdown.

headlines.headline: Thailand scraps 25-year Cambodia energy exploration agreement
headlines.summary: Thailand terminated a quarter-century-old agreement with Cambodia on joint energy exploration in disputed waters, with Phnom Penh now initiating compulsory conciliation under UNCLOS.

headlines.headline: Pakistan PM calls for ceasefire respect after Iran attacks UAE
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif urged respect for the fragile ceasefire as Pakistan continues mediation efforts between the US and Iran following Iranian attacks on the UAE.

headlines.headline: Russia claims to have shot down nearly 300 Ukrainian drones overnight
headlines.summary: The Russian Defense Ministry said it intercepted approximately 300 Ukrainian drones in a massive overnight attack, one of the largest drone barrages of the war.

headlines.headline: UK sanctions target Russia fighter recruitment and drone production
headlines.summary: Britain announced sanctions against 35 individuals and entities involved in recruiting migrants to fight for Russia and in Iranian drone production for the war in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Iran War Gives Modest Boost to Thermal Coal Prices
headlines.summary: Seaborne thermal coal prices in Asia rallied after the US-Israel war on Iran began, but gains remain modest compared to the spike seen during Russia invasion of Ukraine, with analysts noting limited upside.

headlines.headline: Sweden arrests Chinese captain of seized Russia-linked tanker
headlines.summary: Swedish authorities arrested the Chinese captain of a seized oil tanker with suspected Russian links on charges of false documents and seaworthiness violations. The arrest highlights ongoing European enforcement against sanctions evasion.

perspectives.angle: Sweden Arrests Chinese Captain of Russia-Linked Oil Tanker
