perspectives.summary: Swedish authorities arrested the Chinese captain of a seized oil tanker with suspected Russian ties on charges of carrying false documents and violating seaworthiness laws, highlighting continued sanctions enforcement.

headlines.headline: Germany's Merz faces deepest transatlantic crisis in decades
headlines.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz marks one year in office confronting the most serious US-Germany rift in decades, as Trump threatens 25% auto tariffs and withdraws thousands of troops from Germany.

headlines.headline: US to proceed with 25% EU car tariff hike
headlines.summary: The US Trade Representative confirmed Washington will move forward with Trump's plan to raise EU auto import tariffs to 25%, deepening trade tensions with European allies already reeling from troop withdrawal announcements.

headlines.headline: Japan defense chief visits Philippines for security cooperation
headlines.summary: Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi traveled to the Philippines to witness international combat drills and deepen security ties, as Japan expands defense partnerships in the Indo-Pacific region.

perspectives.angle: Japan and Australia Deepen Cooperation on Energy Security and Critical Minerals
perspectives.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements on energy security, defense, and critical minerals cooperation amid the Iran war threatening global supply chains. Japan relies on Australia for nearly half its LNG, and both nations aim to reduce vulnerability to Middle East disruptions.

headlines.headline: India condemns Iran's Fujairah attack as oil prices surge
headlines.summary: India condemned Iran's attack on the UAE's Fujairah oil hub that injured three Indian nationals, urging diplomacy as Middle East tensions drive oil prices higher.

perspectives.angle: India condemns Fujairah attack after three nationals injured in Iranian drone strike
perspectives.summary: India expressed strong condemnation after three Indian citizens were injured in an Iranian drone attack on the UAE port of Fujairah, calling the incident "unacceptable" and urging peaceful resolution of the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Australia's central bank delivers third straight rate hike to 4.35%
headlines.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia raised rates for the third time this year, citing sticky inflation and the Middle East conflict's impact on global oil prices.

headlines.headline: Audi pledges cost cuts as Trump tariff plan threatens outlook
headlines.summary: Volkswagen's Audi is pushing to become leaner as the German carmaker tries to offset waning sales in China and potential tariff impacts from Trump's trade policies.

headlines.headline: US Launches Operation to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Under Fire
headlines.summary: American forces began escorting commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz as Iranian attacks on UAE territory and shipping intensify, straining an already fragile ceasefire arrangement.

perspectives.angle: South Korean ship struck by explosion and fire in Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Seoul confirmed that a South Korean vessel was hit by an explosion and fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, as Iran launched attacks on shipping in the strategic waterway. The incident underscores the widening impact of the US-Iran conflict on global commercial traffic.

perspectives.angle: US-Led Task Force Begins Effort to Reopen Strait of Hormuz, Ships Told to Reroute
perspectives.summary: The US launched an effort to guide stranded ships through the Iran-controlled Strait of Hormuz on Monday, as Iran warned against any interference in the waterway.

headlines.headline: Hormuz Crisis Enters Day 67 with No End in Sight
headlines.summary: The Iran-US conflict continues to deepen as Iran maintains its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, attacks on UAE infrastructure persist, and global oil markets remain disrupted with prices well above  per barrel.

headlines.headline: Putin and Zelenskiy announce rival ceasefires for Victory Day commemorations
headlines.summary: President Putin declared a two-day ceasefire on May 8-9 to mark Russia WWII Victory Day, while Zelenskiy countered with an earlier proposed ceasefire starting May 5-6. Putin warned of massive missile strikes on Kyiv if Victory Day celebrations are disrupted.

perspectives.angle: Russia and Ukraine Announce Rival Ceasefires as Strikes Continue
perspectives.summary: Russia announced a ceasefire for May 8-9 while Ukraine proposed its own pause. The competing offers come as deadly strikes continue to hit Ukrainian infrastructure, with both sides accusing the other of cynicism.

headlines.headline: Australia raises rates for third time this year as oil shock bites
headlines.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia lifted rates to 4.35%, warning inflation would remain sticky as the Middle East conflict unleashed a global oil shock. Headline inflation is now seen peaking near 5%, with growth downgrades expected.

headlines.headline: Apple in Talks with Intel and Samsung for US Chip Production
headlines.summary: Apple has held exploratory discussions about using Intel and Samsung Electronics to produce main processors for its devices in the United States, as part of efforts to diversify its chip supply chain.

headlines.headline: Trump Calls on South Korea to Join Hormuz Protection Mission
headlines.summary: President Trump said South Korea should join US efforts to protect ships near Iran, with Defense Secretary Hegseth scheduled to hold a news conference with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

headlines.headline: Russian Court Freezes Assets of Rusagro Billionaire Founder
headlines.summary: A Russian court has frozen the assets of Vadim Moshkovich, billionaire founder of agricultural producer Rusagro, in what could be another high-profile asset grab by the state.

headlines.headline: Trump Family Crypto Venture Countersues Justin Sun for Defamation
headlines.summary: World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture co-founded by President Trump and his sons, filed a defamation lawsuit against Hong Kong-based crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun, escalating a dispute with one of its most prominent backers.

headlines.headline: Gulf Markets Rise on Hopes of Middle East Resolution
headlines.summary: Most Gulf stock exchanges closed slightly higher, supported by limited signs that the Middle East conflict may be resolved.

perspectives.angle: Gulf Markets Rise on Hopes for Middle East Resolution
perspectives.summary: Most Gulf stock exchanges closed slightly higher Monday, supported by limited signs that the Middle East conflict may be resolved, even as global markets remain jittery.
